# feat(dataflow): Phase 5.9 — multi-webhook-source adapters

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `311693025264` — feat(dataflow): Phase 5.9 — multi-webhook-source adapters

**Body**:

WebhookConfig gains a ``provider`` field selecting one of five
verification schemes (generic, github, gitlab, stripe, slack), and
WebhookReceiver dispatches signature checks through provider-specific
verifier classes instead of hardcoding the legacy SHA256 hex contract.

config.py:
- WebhookConfig.provider: str = "generic" with __post_init__ allowlist
  validation against the canonical 5-provider set
- Per-provider docstring entries pointing at each upstream signing
  contract (GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, Slack documentation)

webhooks.py:
- _SignatureVerifier ABC + 5 implementations:
  * _GenericVerifier — X-Webhook-Signature SHA256 hex (legacy default)
  * _GitHubVerifier — X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=<hex> with mandatory
    prefix; uses X-GitHub-Delivery as the dedup nonce
  * _GitLabVerifier — X-Gitlab-Token plain shared-secret with
    constant-time comparison; X-Gitlab-Event-UUID as nonce
  * _StripeVerifier — Stripe-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>[,v1=...]
    signed over `${t}.${body}` with a 5-minute window check; supports
    multiple v1 candidates for key-rotation grace; body-hash nonce
  * _SlackVerifier — X-Slack-Signature: v0=<hex> over
    `v0:${ts}:${body}` with 5-minute window; body-hash nonce
- _VerifyResult dataclass carries (accepted, reason, nonce) so each
  verifier can pick the most reliable per-provider dedup key
- handle_webhook refactored to dispatch to the verifier table; the
  legacy generic-only X-Webhook-Timestamp window is now scoped to
  the generic provider so providers with their own windows
  (Stripe / Slack) don't get double-checked
- fabric_webhook_received_total metric recorded on every dispatch

tests (25 new in tests/unit/fabric/test_webhook_providers.py):
- Per-verifier accept/reject coverage for each provider
- Receiver-level dispatch for each provider via WebhookConfig
- Cross-provider rejection: a github-configured receiver MUST NOT
  accept the legacy X-Webhook-Signature header

Pre-existing test mock drift fixed in the same commit (zero-tolerance
Rule 1) — these mocks were silently broken on the dataflow-2.0
branch before this phase touched them:
- test_serving.py: _MockPipeline.get_cached now async + accepts
  params/tenant_id kwargs so the new serving-layer cache call shape
  works against the stub
- test_health.py: _MockPipeline gains async get_cached/get_metadata/
  scan_product_metadata; tests marked async because get_health is
  now async (Phase 5.5 wiring)
- test_express_pagination.py: db._trust_executor and
  db._classification_policy explicitly None so MagicMock attribute
  recursion doesn't trip Phase 5.10/5.11 read-path wiring; sync
  test now closes the AsyncMock coroutine to silence RuntimeWarning;
  test_list_accepts_order_by_parameter switched from AsyncMock to a
  real async wrapper so the inner _list() coroutine gets awaited
- test_mcp_integration.py: ImportError test now monkeypatches
  sys.modules["kailash_mcp"] = None because the package is now
  installed in the dev venv

Total: 471 passing across regression/, fabric/, unit/adapters/
test_safe_identifier.py, and unit/fabric/ (was 219 before this
session — Phase 5.9 + Phase 5.12 + Phase 6.4 + drift fixes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
